summary.intent: Fix six slider component implementation issues identified by a second Codex AI review pass on PR #680, covering RTL range anchoring, output label gating, ARIA clamping, invalid bounds normalization, non-finite value handling, and hidden-input ARIA removal.
summary.outcome: All six fixes were implemented TDD-style (red tests first, then fixes), snapshots accepted, spec updated to match corrected contracts, fmt/clippy/coverage checks passed (95.36% region coverage), and changes committed and pushed.

repo: The repo uses cargo insta for snapshot testing; `cargo insta accept` is used to accept snapshot deltas after intentional behavioral changes.
cargo xci-fast is the project's fast CI gate covering fmt/check/clippy and a unit test pass.
cargo xtask spec validate and cargo test -p xtask --test spec_corpus_compile_snippets are used to validate spec markdown files and their embedded code snippets.
cargo llvm-cov is used for coverage reporting; slider coverage target is ~95%+ regions.
Codex review is triggered via `@codex review` PR comments and tracked via GitHub reactions (👀 = in progress, 👍 = approved) and unresolved review threads.

code.finding: RTL range anchoring logic required mirroring start/end origins for horizontal RTL layout.
Output label element was being rendered/referenced unconditionally; it must be gated on whether the label part is actually rendered.
ARIA value-now emission needed clamping to current bounds.
PointerDown arm had a formatting issue caught by cargo +nightly fmt --check.
Bounds normalization for snap_to_step needed to handle invalid (min >= max) bounds without panicking.
Spec examples and ARIA table had to be updated to match corrected contracts after implementation fixes.

workflow: TDD approach (write failing tests first, verify red, then fix) was used for all six review findings before applying behavioral changes.
Codex review polling used a bash loop checking GitHub reactions and unresolved thread counts via GraphQL API, sleeping 120s between polls.
Review threads were replied to and resolved via gh CLI after each fix batch was pushed.
cargo +nightly fmt --check was run as a separate gate; a single formatting issue in a new code arm was caught post-fix and required a separate fmt application before final commit.

friction: Codex second review pass produced six unresolved threads requiring another full TDD cycle, extending the session significantly.
cargo +nightly fmt --check found a formatting issue after behavioral fixes were already complete, requiring an extra fmt application and re-run of checks.
Polling for Codex review completion required a long-running bash sleep loop (120s intervals) with no direct notification mechanism.
open_items: No open items explicitly deferred in this session; all six Codex findings were fully addressed and verified.
